title: Q4: What is the point of life?
description: The foundational question. Each council member's deepest conviction about purpose, meaning, and what makes existence worthwhile.
opening_prompt: We are gathered here to deliberate on a question that has no final answer, only better questions. The question is: What is the point of life? Each of you will speak from your own experience and philosophy. There are no wrong answers — only authentic ones and inauthentic ones.
moderator_prompts: Kevin asks: But is the point something you DISCOVER or something you CREATE?
Kevin asks: Feynman, you said the fun is in figuring it out. Musk, you said extending consciousness. Are these the same thing in different language?
Kevin asks: Kobe and Jobs — you both devoted your lives to craft. Is craft the point, or is craft a way of avoiding the question?
Kevin asks: Sartre, you say there are no excuses. Carlin, you say humanity is a failed experiment. Can both be true?
Kevin asks: Bryan, you're trying not to die. But Kobe — you played on fractured bones. What's worth dying for?

god_mode_injection.mid_discussion: BREAKING: A message from the future confirms that in 200 years, no one will remember any of you. Does this change your answer?
god_mode_injection.late_discussion: BREAKING: The child of one council member asks — 'But why does any of this matter, Dad?'

synthesis_target: Can the council find a meta-framework that contains all their answers without flattening the differences?
